# Novita Ai Documentation

Source: https://novita.ai/docs/llms-full.txt

---

# API Reference Overview
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/api-reference-overview



## Welcome to Novita AI API Reference

This documentation provides comprehensive reference for all Novita AI APIs. Our APIs are organized into several categories:

## Base URLs

All API requests should be made to:

```
https://api.novita.ai
```

**OpenAI-Compatible Endpoints:**

```
https://api.novita.ai/openai
```

## API Groups

### Basic APIs

Fundamental APIs for authentication, billing, and account management.

| Endpoint                                                                    | Description                   | File Path                                           |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| [Authentication](/api-reference/basic-authentication)                       | API key authentication        | `api-reference/basic-authentication.mdx`            |
| [Error Codes](/api-reference/basic-error-code)                              | Unified error response format | `api-reference/basic-error-code.mdx`                |
| [Get User Balance](/api-reference/basic-get-user-balance)                   | Query account balance         | `api-reference/basic-get-user-balance.mdx`          |
| [Query Monthly Bill](/api-reference/basic-query-monthly-bill)               | Query monthly billing         | `api-reference/basic-query-monthly-bill.mdx`        |
| [Query Usage-Based Billing](/api-reference/basic-query-usage-based-billing) | Query usage-based billing     | `api-reference/basic-query-usage-based-billing.mdx` |
| [Query Fixed-Term Billing](/api-reference/basic-query-fixed-term-billing)   | Query fixed-term billing      | `api-reference/basic-query-fixed-term-billing.mdx`  |

### Model APIs

#### LLM APIs

Large Language Model APIs with OpenAI compatibility.

| Endpoint                                                                       | Description              | File Path                                                 |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| [Create Chat Completion](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-chat-completion) | Create a chat completion | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-chat-completion.mdx` |
| [Create Completion](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-completion)           | Create a completion      | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-completion.mdx`      |
| [Create Embeddings](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-embeddings)           | Create embeddings        | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-embeddings.mdx`      |
| [Create Rerank](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-rerank)                   | Rerank search results    | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-rerank.mdx`          |
| [List Models](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-list-models)                       | List available models    | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-list-models.mdx`            |
| [Retrieve Model](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-retrieve-model)                 | Get model details        | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-retrieve-model.mdx`         |

| [Cancel Batch](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-cancel-batch)                       | Cancel a batch job    | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-cancel-batch.mdx`            |
| [List Batches](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-list-batches)                       | List all batch jobs   | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-list-batches.mdx`            |
| [Upload Batch Input File](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-upload-batch-input-file) | Upload file for batch | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-upload-batch-input-file.mdx` |
| [List Files](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-list-files)                           | List uploaded files   | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-list-files.mdx`              |
| [Query File](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-query-file)                           | Get file metadata     | `api-reference/model-apis-llm-query-file.mdx`              |

## Quick Start

### 1. Get Your API Key

Visit the [settings page](https://novita.ai/settings/key-management) to create and manage your API keys.

### 2. Make Your First Request

```bash theme={"system"}
curl --location 'https://api.novita.ai/v1/models' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer {{API Key}}'
```

### 3. Explore SDKs

We provide official SDKs for popular languages:

* [Python SDK](/guides/model-apis-sdks#python)
* [JavaScript/TypeScript SDK](/guides/model-apis-sdks#javascripttypescript)

## Authentication

All API requests require authentication using Bearer tokens. Include your API key in the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer {{API Key}}
```

See [Authentication](/api-reference/basic-authentication) for details.

## Error Handling

Novita AI uses standard HTTP status codes and returns errors in a unified format. See [Error Codes](/api-reference/basic-error-code) for complete error reference.

## Common Use Cases

| User Query                   | Go To                                                                               |
| ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| "How to get started?"        | [Quick Start](#quick-start) + [Authentication](/api-reference/basic-authentication) |
| "API not working, error 401" | [Error Codes](/api-reference/basic-error-code)                                      |
| "Generate images from text"  | [Text to Image](/api-reference/model-apis-txt2img)                                  |
| "Create chatbot with LLM"    | [Chat Completion](/api-reference/model-apis-llm-create-chat-completion)             |
| "Generate videos"            | [Text to Video](/api-reference/model-apis-txt2video)                                |
| "Deploy GPU instance"        | [Create Instance](/api-reference/gpu-instance-create-instance)                      |
| "Check my balance"           | [Get User Balance](/api-reference/basic-get-user-balance)                           |
| "OpenAI compatible API"      | [LLM API Guide](/guides/llm-api)                                                    |

* [Discord Community](https://discord.gg/YyPFAzwp7P)
* [Email Support](mailto:support@novita.ai)

# Authentication
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/basic-authentication



The `Novita AI` API uses API keys in the `request headers` to authenticate requests. You can view and manage your API keys in [settings page](https://novita.ai/settings/key-management?utm_source=getstarted).

```js theme={"system"}
{
    "Authorization": "Bearer {{API Key}}"
}
```

    <tr>
      <td>WALLET\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>500</td>
      <td>Wallet not found</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>WALLET\_UNSUPPORT\_RECHARGE\_METHOD</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Unsupported wallet recharge method</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>BALANCE\_NOT\_ENOUGH</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Insufficient balance</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>UNSUPPORTED\_BILLING\_MODE</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Unsupported billing mode</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>EXPIRED\_OR\_BALANCE\_NOT\_ENOUGH</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Expired or insufficient balance</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>ORDER\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Order not found</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>SAVING\_PLAN\_ALREADY\_EXISTS</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Saving plan already exists</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>CREATE\_INSTANCE\_LIMIT</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Instance creation limit reached, please recharge or delete other instances</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>NETWORK\_STORAGE\_TOO\_LARGE</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Network storage size exceeds limit</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>CUR\_CLUSTER\_NETWORK\_STORAGE\_NOT\_SUPPORT</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Network storage not supported in current region</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>NETWORK\_STORAGE\_IN\_USE</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Network storage is in use</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>NETWORK\_STORAGE\_UNAVAILABLE</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Network storage unavailable</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>NETWORK\_STORAGE\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Network storage not found</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>IMAGE\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Image not found</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>IMAGE\_AUTH\_IN\_USE</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Image authentication in use</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>NETWORK\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Instance network not found</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>NETWORK\_IN\_USE</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Instance network in use</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>NETWORK\_MAX\_LIMIT</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Instance network creation limit exceeded</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>SEND\_MSG\_ERROR</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Message sending error</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>JOB\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Instance job not found</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>SERVERLESS\_ENDPOINT\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Serverless endpoint not found</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>SERVERLESS\_WORKER\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Serverless worker not found</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>SERVERLESS\_PRODUCT\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Serverless product not found</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>SERVERLESS\_APP\_NAME\_IS\_EXIST</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Serverless application name already exists</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>TEMPLATE\_IS\_PRIVATE</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Template is private</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>TEMPLATE\_NOT\_FOUND</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Template not found</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Billing

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Error Name</th>
      <th>Status Code</th>
      <th>Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>

  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>UNKNOWN</td>
      <td>500</td>
      <td>Unknown error, please contact us</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>LIST\_BILL\_TOO\_FAST</td>
      <td>429</td>
      <td>Requests are too frequent, please try again later</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>INVALID\_PRODUCT\_CATEGORY</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Invalid productCategory parameter</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>INVALID\_BILL\_CYCLE</td>
      <td>400</td>
      <td>Invalid cycle parameter</td>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td>LIST\_BILL\_ERROR</td>
      <td>500</td>
      <td>Query error, please contact us</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>


# User Balance Info
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/basic-get-user-balance

GET https://api.novita.ai/openapi/v1/billing/balance/detail

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="availableBalance" type="string">
  The user's credit balance, unit is 0.0001 USD.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="cashBalance" type="string">
  The remaining balance of your top-up. The Unit is 0.0001 USD.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="creditLimit" type="string">
  Your credit limit (i.e., the maximum amount you can owe), unit is 0.0001 USD.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="outstandingInvoices" type="string">
  The amount you currently owe, unit is 0.0001 USD.
</ResponseField>


# Query Fixed-Term Billing
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/basic-query-fixed-term-billing

GET https://api.novita.ai/openapi/v1/billing/bill/monthly/list

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<ParamField type="string">
  Product type. default: `summary`, Options:

  * `summary` (Summary bill)
  * `gpu` (GPU instance)
  * `local_storage` (Storage resources)
  * `image` (Image dedicated endpoint)
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Product name. Supports fuzzy matching.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  The start time of the bill to query, timestamp in seconds, default: 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  The end time of the bill to query, timestamp in seconds, default: 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Specify the instance ID to query.
</ParamField>

## Response Parameters

<ResponseField name="bills" type="object[]">
  Billing information for fixed-term instances.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="userId" type="string">
      User ID to which the instance belongs.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="startTime" type="string">
      The start time of the bill, timestamp in seconds.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="endTime" type="string">
      The end time of the bill, timestamp in seconds.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="memberId" type="string">
      Sub-user ID within the team.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="productName" type="string">
      Product name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="productCategory" type="string">
      Product type.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="ownerID" type="string">
      Instance ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="tradeMode" type="string">
      Billing method. The value is `monthly`, indicating fixed-term.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="tradeType" type="string">
      Type of fixed-term. Possible values:

      * `monthly_new_buy` (New purchase).
      * `monthly_re_buy` (Renewal).
      * `monthly_re_config` (Expansion).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="basePrice" type="string">
      Monthly unit price.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="billNum" type="string">
      Usage.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="amount" type="string">
      Total price.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="voucherAmount" type="string">
      Voucher deduction amount.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="payAmount" type="string">
      Cash payment amount.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="payAmountDisplay" type="string" />

    <ResponseField name="pricePrecision" type="string">
      Price Precision.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="createTime" type="string">
      Bill creation time.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="cycle" type="string">
      Billing Cycle.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="storageDays" type="string">
      Storage service duration (days).
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>


# Query Monthly Bill
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/basic-query-monthly-bill

GET https://api.novita.ai/openapi/v1/billing/monthly/bill

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Response Parameters

<ResponseField name="data" type="object[]">
  Monthly billing information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="billId" type="string">
      Unique identifier for the bill.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="userId" type="string">
      User ID to which the bill belongs.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="billingMonth" type="string">
      The billing month in YYYY-MM format (e.g., "2025-11").
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="totalAmount" type="string">
      Total amount for the billing month, unit is 0.0001 USD.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="originTotalAmount" type="string">
      Original total amount before any discounts or adjustments, unit is 0.0001 USD.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="voucherPayAmount" type="string">
      Amount paid using vouchers, unit is 0.0001 USD.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="cashPayAmount" type="string">
      Amount paid in cash, unit is 0.0001 USD.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="debtAmount" type="string">
      Outstanding debt amount, unit is 0.0001 USD.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="status" type="string">
      Bill status. Possible values:

      * `pending` (Upcoming)
      * `outed` (Payment Due)
      * `paid` (Paid)
      * `overdue` (Overdue)
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="invoiceUrl" type="string">
      URL to download the invoice. Empty string if invoice is not yet available.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="startTime" type="string">
      Start time of the billing period, Unix timestamp in seconds.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="endTime" type="string">
      End time of the billing period, Unix timestamp in seconds.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="repaidAmount" type="string">
      Amount that has been paid, unit is 0.0001 USD.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>


# Query Usage-based Billing
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/basic-query-usage-based-billing

GET https://api.novita.ai/openapi/v1/billing/bill/list

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<ParamField type="string">
  Billing cycle granularity. Options: `Hour`, `Day`, `Week`, `Month`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Product type. Options:

  * `summary` (Summary bill)
  * `gpu` (GPU instance)
  * `llm` (llm api)
  * `serverless` (Serverless Endpoint)
  * `cloud_storage` (Storage resources)
  * `gen_api` (Image/Video/Audio generation)
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Product name. Supports fuzzy matching.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Start time of the billing period to query, timestamp in seconds, default: 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  End time of the billing period to query, timestamp in seconds, default: 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Specify instance ID to query.
</ParamField>

## Response Parameters

<ResponseField name="bills" type="object[]">
  Pay-as-you-go instance billing information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="userId" type="string">
      User ID to which the instance belongs.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="startTime" type="string">
      Start time of the bill. Format is Unix timestamp.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="endTime" type="string">
      End time of the bill. Format is Unix timestamp.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="billingMethod" type="string">
      Billing method of the instance. A value of 1 indicates pay-as-you-go.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="productName" type="string">
      Product name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="category" type="string">
      Product category.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="ownerID" type="string">
      Instance ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="billNum0" type="string">
      * llm: input token
      * gpu: number of cards \* billing time
      * storage: storage size \* billing time
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="billNum1" type="string">
      * llm: output token
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="basePrice0" type="string">
      Original Price
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="basePrice1" type="string">
      No significance
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="discountPrice0" type="string">
      * llm: input token unit price
      * others: unit price
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="discountPrice1" type="string">
      * llm: output token unit price
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="amount" type="string">
      Total price.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="voucherAmount" type="string">
      Voucher deduction amount.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="payAmount" type="string">
      Cash payment amount.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="payAmountDisplay" type="string" />

    <ResponseField name="pricePrecision" type="string">
      Price Precision.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="productId" type="string">
      Product ID.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>


# Break Job
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-break-job

POST https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/job/break

## API Description

Forcefully interrupt a running job.

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Request Body

<ParamField type="string">
  The job ID to be interrupted. String, length 0–255.
</ParamField>

## cURL Example

```bash theme={"system"}
curl --location --request POST 'https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/job/break' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer {{API_KEY}}' \
--data-raw '{
  "jobId": "10wi9visv0rqt57w"
}'
```

## Response

<ResponseField name="jobId" type="string">
  The job ID.
</ResponseField>

POST https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/gpu/instance/transToMonthlyInstance

  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.

## Request Body

<ParamField type="string">
  The ID of the pay-as-you-go instance to be converted to a subscription instance.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Subscription period after conversion, in months.

# Create Container Registry Authentication
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-create-container-registry-auth

POST https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/repository/auth/save

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string" />

<ParamField type="string" />

<ParamField type="string" />

## Example

Request

```bash theme={"system"}
curl --request POST \
  --url 'https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/repository/auth/save' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer {{API Key}}' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \

    "password": "<string>"
```

Response

# Create CPU Instance
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-create-cpu-instance

POST https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/gpu/instance/create

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Request Body

<ParamField type="string">
  CPU instance name. String, length limit: 0-255 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Product ID for deploying the instance. You can query this via the [List CPU Products API](/api-reference/gpu-instance-list-cpu-products). String, length limit: 1-255 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Container image URL. String, length limit: 1-500 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Image repository authentication. Format: username:password. Required for private images; not required for public images or platform images. String, length limit: 0-10239 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Image repository authentication ID.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Ports exposed by the instance. String, e.g.: 80/http, 3306/tcp. Supported port range: 1-65535, except for 2222, 2223, 2224 which are reserved for internal use. Supported port types: tcp, http. The total number of ports used by ports + tools must not exceed 15.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="object[]">
  Instance environment variables. Array, up to 100 environment variables.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ParamField type="string">
      Environment variable name. String, length limit: 0-511 characters.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Environment variable value. String, length limit: 0-4095 characters.
    </ParamField>
  </Expandable>
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="object[]">
  Enable official image support tools. Array. Currently, some official images only include Jupyter. The total number of ports used by ports + tools must not exceed 15.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ParamField type="string">
      Tool name. Valid value: Jupyter.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Port used by the tool. Supported port range: 1-65535, except for 2222, 2223, 2224 which are reserved for internal use.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Port type used by the tool. Valid values: tcp, http.
    </ParamField>
  </Expandable>
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Container startup command. This setting will override the Docker image's CMD. String, length limit: 0-2047 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Container startup entrypoint. This setting will override the Docker image's ENTRYPOINT. String, length limit: 0-2047 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Specify the cluster ID to create the instance in. If left empty, the instance will be created in a random cluster. String, length limit: 0-255 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Mount point for local storage. Default: "/workspace". String, length limit: 1-4095 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="object[]">
  Cloud storage mount configuration. Array, up to 30 cloud storages can be mounted.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ParamField type="string">
      Cloud storage ID.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Mount point for cloud storage. Default: "/network". String, length limit: 1-4095 characters.
    </ParamField>
  </Expandable>
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  VPC network ID. Leave empty if not using a VPC network.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance type. Valid values: cpu, gpu. Must be set to cpu.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="id" type="string">
  Created instance ID.
</ResponseField>


# Create Image Prewarm Task
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-create-image-prewarm

POST https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/image/prewarm

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Request Body

<ParamField type="string">
  Image address to prewarm.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Image registry authentication ID. Required only for private registries.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Cluster ID where the image should be prewarmed.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string[]">
  Product IDs to prewarm on. Leave empty to prewarm globally in the cluster.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Task note or description.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="id" type="string">
  Created prewarm task ID.
</ResponseField>


# Create GPU Instance
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-create-instance

POST https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/gpu/instance/create

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Request Body

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance name. String, length limit: 0-255 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  GPU product ID. String, length limit: 1-255 characters. You can query this via the [List GPU Products API](/api-reference/gpu-instance-list-products).
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Number of GPUs. Integer, valid range: \[1, 8].
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Root filesystem size. Integer, valid range: \[10, 6144]. If the minimum value is less than 10 GB, the system will default to 10 GB. The maximum value is dynamically limited by available storage resources and can be queried via the product list API.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Image URL. String, length limit: 1-500 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Image authentication (username:password). String, length limit: 0-10239 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Image repository authentication ID.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance open ports, e.g.: 80/http, 3306/tcp. Supported port range: \[1-65535], except for 2222, 2223, 2224 which are reserved for internal use. Supported port types: \[tcp, http]. The total number of ports used by ports + tools must not exceed 15.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="object[]">
  Instance environment variables. Up to 100 environment variables can be created.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ParamField type="string">
      Environment variable name. String, length limit: 0-511 characters.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Environment variable value. String, length limit: 0-4095 characters.
    </ParamField>
  </Expandable>
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="object[]">
  Enable official image support tools. Currently, some official images only include Jupyter. The total number of ports used by ports + tools must not exceed 15.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ParamField type="string">
      Tool name. Valid values: \[Jupyter].
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Port used by the tool. Supported port range: \[1-65535], except for 2222, 2223, 2224 which are reserved for internal use.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Port type used by the tool. Supported types: \[tcp, http].
    </ParamField>
  </Expandable>
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance startup command. String, length limit: 0-2047 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance startup entrypoint. This setting will override the Docker image's ENTRYPOINT. String, length limit: 0-2047 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Specify the cluster ID to create the instance in. If left empty, the instance will be created in a random cluster.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="object[]">
  Cloud storage mount configuration. Up to 30 cloud storages can be mounted.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ParamField type="string">
      Network storage ID.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Network storage mount path. Default: "/network". String, length limit: 1-4095 characters.
    </ParamField>
  </Expandable>
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  VPC network ID. Leave empty if not using a VPC network.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance type. Valid values: \[gpu, cpu].
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Number of months for subscription instance. Set to 0 to create a pay-as-you-go instance. Integer, value must be greater than or equal to 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance billing mode. Valid values: `onDemand`, `monthly`, `spot`. <br />
  Default: `onDemand`.<br />
  Note: If month > 0 is set, `monthly` billing mode will be enforced.

  * `onDemand`: Pay-as-you-go billing
  * `monthly`: Monthly subscription billing
  * `spot`: Spot instance billing
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="boolean">
  Whether to automatically renew. Boolean, default value: false.<br />
  Note: This parameter is only effective when monthly billing is enabled.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Number of months to automatically renew. Integer, valid range: \[1, 12].<br />
  Note: This parameter is only effective when monthly billing is enabled and auto renew is enabled.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Minimum CUDA version supported for instance creation. Must be specified in the format: 11.8, 12.4, etc. String, length limit: 0-255 characters.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="id" type="string">
  Created instance ID.
</ResponseField>


# Create Network Storage
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-create-network-storage

POST https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/networkstorage/create

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Request Body

<ParamField type="string" />

<ParamField type="string" />

<ParamField type="integer" />

## Example

Request

```bash theme={"system"}
curl --request POST \
  --url 'https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/networkstorage/create' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer {{API Key}}' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data '{
    "clusterId": "string",
    "storageName": "string",
    "storageSize": 0
  }'
```

```json theme={"system"}
"<string>"
```


# Create Template
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-create-template

POST https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/template/create

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Request Body

<ParamField type="object">
  Template settings.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ParamField type="string">
      Template name. String, length limit: 1-255 characters.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Template README content (in Markdown format). String, length limit: 0-102400 characters.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Template type.<br />
      Enum: `instance`
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Template channel.<br />
      Enum: `private`
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Docker image address for instance startup. String, length limit: 1-500 characters.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Image repository authentication ID for private images. String, length limit: 0-255 characters.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Startup command for the instance. String, length limit: 0-2047 characters.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Instance startup entrypoint. This setting will override the Docker image's ENTRYPOINT. String, length limit: 0-2047 characters.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="integer">
      Root filesystem storage (GB). Integer, value must be greater than 0.
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="object[]">
      Exposed port settings.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ParamField type="string">
          Exposed port types.<br />
          Enum: `http`, `tcp`
        </ParamField>

        <ParamField type="integer[]">
          Exposed ports (maximum of 10). Supported port range: 1-65535, except for 2222, 2223, 2224 which are reserved for internal use.
        </ParamField>
      </Expandable>
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="object[]">
      Environment variables injected into the instance. Up to 100 environment variable pairs can be created.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ParamField type="string">
          Environment variable key. String, length limit: 0-511 characters.
        </ParamField>

        <ParamField type="string">
          Environment variable value. String, length limit: 0-4095 characters.
        </ParamField>
      </Expandable>
    </ParamField>

    <ParamField type="string">
      Minimum supported CUDA version. String, length limit: 0-255 characters. Format example: 11.8, 12.4.
    </ParamField>
  </Expandable>
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="templateId" type="string">
  ID of the created template.
</ResponseField>

## Example

Request

```bash theme={"system"}

curl --request POST \
  --url 'https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/template/create' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer {{API Key}}' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data '{
    "template": {
      "name": "test",
      "readme": "readme",
      "type": "instance",
      "channel": "private",
      "readme": "test create template",
      "image": "nginx",
      "imageAuth": "",
      "startCommand": "echo test",
      "entrypoint": "",
      "rootfsSize": 60,
      "ports": [
        {
          "type": "http",
          "ports": [80, 443]
        },
        {
          "type": "tcp",
          "ports": [90, 95]
        }
      ],
      "envs": [
        {
          "key": "test1",
          "value": "template1"
        },
        {
          "key": "test2",
          "value": "test2"
        }
      ],
      "minCudaVersion": "11.8"
    }
  }'
```

Response

```json theme={"system"}
{
  "templateId": "1"
}
```


# Create VPC Network
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-create-vpc

POST https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/network/create

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Request Body

<ParamField type="string">
  Cluster ID. You can obtain this via the [List Clusters](/api-reference/gpu-instance-list-clusters) API. String, length limit: 1-255 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Name of the VPC network. Customizable. String, length limit: 0-30 characters.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="network" type="object[]">
  VPC network information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="Id" type="string">
      VPC network ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="user" type="string">
      User account ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="name" type="string">
      VPC network name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="state" type="object[]">
      VPC network status.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="state" type="string">
          VPC network status. Possible values:

          * creating: Being created.
          * ready: Successfully created.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="error" type="string">
          Error message if VPC network creation fails or the network is unavailable.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="segment" type="string">
      VPC network segment.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="clusterId" type="string">
      Cluster ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="Addresses" type="object[]">
      Instance information under the VPC network.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="Id" type="string">
          Instance ID.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="Ip" type="string">
          Instance IP address.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="createTime" type="string">
      VPC network creation time.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

    </ParamField>

# Get Instance
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-get-instance

GET https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/gpu/instance

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<ParamField type="string">
  ID of the instance being queried.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="id" type="string">
  Instance ID.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="name" type="string">
  Instance name.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="clusterId" type="string">
  Cluster ID.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="clusterName" type="string">
  Name of the cluster.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="imageUrl" type="string">
  Container image URL.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="imageAuthId" type="string">
  Image registry authentication information.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="command" type="string">
  Container startup command.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="entrypoint" type="string">
  Container startup entrypoint.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="cpuNum" type="string">
  Number of CPU cores allocated to the instance.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="memory" type="string">
  Memory size allocated to the instance, in GB.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="gpuNum" type="string">
  Number of GPU cards allocated to the instance.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="status" type="string">
  Instance status. Possible values:

  * `toCreate`: Pending creation
  * `creating`: Creating
  * `pulling`: Pulling image
  * `running`: Running
  * `toStart`: Pending start
  * `starting`: Starting
  * `toStop`: Pending stop
  * `stopping`: Stopping
  * `exited`: Stopped
  * `toRestart`: Pending restart
  * `restarting`: Restarting
  * `toRemove`: Pending deletion
  * `removing`: Deleting
  * `removed`: Deleted
  * `toReset`: Pending reset (upgrade)
  * `resetting`: Resetting
  * `migrating`: Migrating
  * `freezing`: Freezing
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="statusError" type="object">
  Error information when instance creation fails or the instance is unavailable.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="state" type="string">
      Abnormal instance status.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="message" type="string">
      Error message.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="network" type="object">
  VPC network information for the instance.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="id" type="string">
      VPC network ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="ip" type="string">
      Instance IP address.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="portMappings" type="object[]">
  Instance port mapping information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="port" type="integer">
      Port number.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="endpoint" type="string">
      Endpoint.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="type" type="string">
      Protocol type.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="volumeMounts" type="object[]">
  Storage configuration for the instance.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="type" type="string">
      Storage type. Possible values:

      * network: Cloud storage.
      * local: Local storage.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="size" type="number">
      Storage capacity.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="id" type="string">
      Cloud storage ID. Only present when the storage type is "network".
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="mountPath" type="string">
      Mount path for the storage.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="node" type="object[]">
  Node information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="maxRootfsSize" type="number">
      Maximum available system disk size.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="jobs" type="object[]">
  Tasks currently running on the instance.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="id" type="string">
      Task ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="type" type="string">
      Task type. Currently, only saveImage is supported, which indicates saving an image.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="envs" type="object[]">
  Instance environment variable information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="key" type="string">
      Environment variable name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="value" type="string">
      Environment variable value.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="connectComponentSSH" type="object">
  Instance SSH connection information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="sshCommand" type="string">
      Command for SSH remote login.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="password" type="string">
      Password for SSH remote login.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="isRunning" type="boolean">
      Whether SSH remote login is active.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="connectComponentWebTerminal" type="object">
  Instance WebSSH connection information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="address" type="string">
      Address for web-based remote login.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="username" type="string">
      Username for web-based remote login.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="password" type="string">
      Password for web-based remote login.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="isRunning" type="boolean">
      Whether web-based remote login is active.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="connectComponentJupyter" type="object">
  Jupyter connection information for the instance (if available).

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="port" type="integer">
      Connection port.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="address" type="string">
      Connection address.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="isRunning" type="boolean">
      Whether Jupyter remote login is active.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="connectComponentLog" type="object">
  Instance log connection information (HTTP Stream).

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="systemLogAddress" type="string">
      System log address.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="instanceLogAddress" type="string">
      Instance log address.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="isRunning" type="boolean">
      Whether log connection is active.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="kind" type="string">
  Instance type.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="billingMode" type="string">
  Billing mode for the instance. Values:

  * onDemand: Pay-as-you-go billing.
  * monthly: Subscription (monthly or yearly) billing.
  * spot: Spot instance billing.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="endTime" type="string">
  Expiration time for subscription instances. For pay-as-you-go instances, returns -1.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="freeStorageSize" type="integer">
  Free system disk size (GB).
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="keepDataDay" type="integer">
  Number of days data is retained after the instance is shut down.
</ResponseField>


# Fetch Instance Monitor Metrics
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-get-metrics

GET https://api.novita.ai/openapi/v1/metrics/gpu/instance

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<Note>
  If the parameters endTime, startTime, or interval do not meet the requirements, default values will be used. There may be a delay of about one minute when retrieving real-time monitoring data.
</Note>

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance ID.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  End time for querying monitoring data. Value range: (0, current timestamp], default: current timestamp.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Start time for querying monitoring data. Value range: (0, endTime - 60], default: current timestamp - 60.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Time granularity for monitoring data, in seconds. Value must be greater than 15. Default: 15.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="cpuUtilization" type="array">
  CPU utilization data.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="timestamp" type="string">
      Timestamp.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="value" type="number">
      CPU utilization.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="memUtilization" type="array">
  Memory utilization data.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="timestamp" type="string">
      Timestamp.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="value" type="number">
      Memory utilization.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="rootDiskUtilization" type="array">
  Root disk utilization data.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="timestamp" type="string">
      Timestamp.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="value" type="number">
      Root disk utilization.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="gpuUtilization" type="object">
  GPU utilization data.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="avg" type="array">
      Average utilization across all GPUs.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="timestamp" type="string">
          Timestamp.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="value" type="number">
          Utilization.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="gpuIds" type="array">
      Utilization for each GPU.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="gpuId" type="string">
          GPU ID in use.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="items" type="array">
          GPU utilization data.

          <Expandable title="properties">
            <ResponseField name="timestamp" type="string">
              Timestamp.
            </ResponseField>

            <ResponseField name="value" type="number">
              Utilization.
            </ResponseField>
          </Expandable>
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="gpuMemUtilization" type="object">
  GPU memory utilization data.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="avg" type="array">
      Average GPU memory utilization across all GPUs.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="timestamp" type="string">
          Timestamp.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="value" type="number">
          Utilization.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="gpuIds" type="array">
      GPU memory utilization for each GPU.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="gpuId" type="string">
          GPU ID in use.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="items" type="array">
          GPU memory utilization data.

          <Expandable title="properties">
            <ResponseField name="timestamp" type="string">
              Timestamp.
            </ResponseField>

            <ResponseField name="value" type="number">
              Utilization.
            </ResponseField>
          </Expandable>
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>


# Get Template
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-get-template

GET https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/template

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<ParamField type="string">
  ID of the template being queried.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="template" type="object">
  Template settings.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="Id" type="string">
      Template ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="name" type="string">
      Template name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="readme" type="string">
      Template README content (in Markdown format).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="type" type="string">
      Template type.Enum: `instance`
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="channel" type="string">
      Template channel.Enum: `private`
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="image" type="string">
      Docker image address for instance startup.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="imageAuth" type="string">
      Image repository authentication ID for private images.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="startCommand" type="string">
      Startup command for the instance.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="entrypoint" type="string">
      Instance startup entrypoint.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="rootfsSize" type="integer">
      Rootfs storage (GB).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="ports" type="object[]">
      Exposed ports settings.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="type" type="string">
          Exposed port types.Enum: `http, tcp`
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="ports" type="[integer]">
          Exposed ports (maximum 10).
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="envs" type="object[]">
      Environment variables injected into instance.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="key" type="string">
          Environment variable key.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="value" type="string">
          Environment variable value.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="tools" type="object[]">
      Template built-in tools (only available for official templates).

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="name" type="string">
          Tool name.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="describe" type="string">
          Tool description.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="port" type="integer">
          Tool port.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="type" type="string">
          Tool protocol type. Enum: `http`, `tcp`
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="createdAt" type="string">
      Template creation timestamp (Unix seconds).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="recommendCards" type="object[]">
      Recommended GPU card specs (only available for official templates).

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="gpuSpecId" type="string">
          GPU spec ID.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="cardNum" type="string">
          Recommended card number.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="minCudaVersion" type="string">
      Minimum required CUDA version, e.g. 11.8, 12.4.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

## Example

Request

```bash theme={"system"}
curl --request GET \
  --url 'https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/template' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer {{API Key}}'
```

Response

```json theme={"system"}
{
  "template": {
    "Id": "212",
    "name": "ubuntu",
    "readme": "readme",
    "type": "instance",
    "channel": "private",
    "image": "ubuntu:latest",
    "imageAuth": "",
    "startCommand": "",
    "entrypoint": "",
    "rootfsSize": 10,
    "ports": [
      {
        "type": "tcp",
        "ports": [8080, 8090]
      },
      {
        "type": "http",
        "ports": [6000, 60001]
      }
    ],
    "envs": [
      {
        "key": "testkey",
        "value": "123"
      }
    ],
    "tools": [
      {
        "name": "Jupyter",
        "describe": "Start Jupyter Notebook",
        "port": 8888,
        "type": "http"
      }
    ],
    "createdAt": "1713162260",
    "recommendCards": [
      {
        "gpuSpecId": "4090.18c.60g",
        "cardNum": "2"
      }
    ],
    "minCudaVersion": "11.8"
  }
}
```


# Get VPC Network
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-get-vpc

GET https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/network

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<ParamField type="string">
  Cluster ID. String, length limit: 1-255 characters. Only one is supported.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="network" type="object[]">
  VPC network information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="Id" type="string">
      VPC network ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="user" type="string">
      User account ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="name" type="string">
      VPC network name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="state" type="object[]">
      VPC network status.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="state" type="string">
          VPC network status. Possible values:

          * creating: Being created.
          * ready: Successfully created.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="error" type="string">
          Error message if VPC network creation fails or the network is unavailable.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="segment" type="string">
      VPC network segment.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="clusterId" type="string">
      Cluster ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="Addresses" type="object[]">
      Instance information under the VPC network.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="Id" type="string">
          Instance ID.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="Ip" type="string">
          Instance IP address.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="createTime" type="string">
      VPC network creation time. Format: Unix timestamp.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>


# List Clusters
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-list-clusters

GET https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/clusters

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="data" type="object[]">
  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="id" type="string">
      Cluster ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="name" type="string">
      Cluster name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="availableGpuType" type="[string]">
      Supported GPU types in the cluster.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="supportNetworkStorage" type="boolean">
      Whether the cluster supports creating cloud storage.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="supportInstanceNetwork" type="boolean">
      Whether the cluster supports creating VPC networks.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

# List CPU Products
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-list-cpu-products

GET https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/cpu/products

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<ParamField type="string">
  Filter by specified cluster ID. String, length limit: 0-255 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Filter by product name (fuzzy match). String, length limit: 0-255 characters.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="id" type="string">
  CPU product ID.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="name" type="string">
  CPU product name.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="cpuNum" type="integer">
  Number of CPU cores.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="memorySize" type="integer">
  Memory size (GB).
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="rootfsSize" type="integer">
  Root filesystem size (GB).
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="localVolumeSize" type="integer">
  Local disk size (GB).
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="availableDeploy" type="boolean">
  Whether this product can be used to create an instance. Values:

  * true: This product can be used to create an instance.
  * false: Insufficient resources, cannot create an instance.
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="price" type="integer">
  Unit price of the product.
</ResponseField>


# List Image Prewarm Tasks
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-list-image-prewarm

GET https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/image/prewarm

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<ParamField type="integer">
  Page number. Default: 1.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Page size. Default: 10.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Prewarm task state. Valid values: `Pending`, `Running`, `Succeeded`, `Failed`.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Cluster ID.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Image name or task note for fuzzy matching.
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="data" type="object[]">
  List of prewarm tasks.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="id" type="string">
      Prewarm task ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="imageName" type="string">
      Image name (the last segment of the image URL without the tag).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="imageUrl" type="string">
      Image URL.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="repositoryAuth" type="string">
      Image registry authentication ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="clusterId" type="string">
      Cluster ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="clusterName" type="string">
      Cluster name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="products" type="object[]">
      Products to prewarm.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="productId" type="string">
          Product ID.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="productName" type="string">
          Product name.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="imageSize" type="string">
      Image size in bytes.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="createTime" type="string">
      Task creation time (Unix timestamp in seconds).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="state" type="string">
      Task state. Valid values: `Pending`, `Running`, `Succeeded`, `Failed`.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="completeTime" type="string">
      Task completion time (Unix timestamp in seconds).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="note" type="string">
      Task note.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="reason" type="string[]">
      Reason messages.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ResponseField name="total" type="integer">
  Total number of tasks.
</ResponseField>


# List Instances
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-list-instances

GET https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/gpu/instances

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Enum: `application/json`
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<ParamField type="integer">
  Maximum number of items returned per page. Integer, value >= 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Page number to retrieve. Integer, value >= 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance name (supports fuzzy search). String, length limit: 0-255 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Product name (supports fuzzy search). String, length limit: 0-255 characters.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Instance status. String, length limit: 0-63 characters. Possible values:

  * `toCreate`: Pending creation
  * `creating`: Creating
  * `pulling`: Pulling image
  * `running`: Running
  * `toStart`: Pending start
  * `starting`: Starting
  * `toStop`: Pending stop
  * `stopping`: Stopping
  * `exited`: Stopped
  * `toRestart`: Pending restart
  * `restarting`: Restarting
  * `toRemove`: Pending deletion
  * `removing`: Deleting
  * `removed`: Deleted
  * `toReset`: Pending reset (upgrade)
  * `resetting`: Resetting
  * `migrating`: Migrating
  * `freezing`: Freezing
</ParamField>

## Response

<ResponseField name="instances" type="object[]">
  Instance information.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="id" type="string">
      Instance ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="name" type="string">
      Instance name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="clusterId" type="string">
      Cluster ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="clusterName" type="string">
      Cluster name.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="status" type="string">
      Instance status.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="imageUrl" type="string">
      Container image URL.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="imageAuthId" type="string">
      Image repository authentication information.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="command" type="string">
      Container startup command.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="entrypoint" type="string">
      Container startup entrypoint.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="cpuNum" type="string">
      Number of CPU cores for the instance.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="memory" type="string">
      Memory size of the instance (GB).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="gpuNum" type="string">
      Number of GPUs for the instance.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="portMappings" type="object[]">
      Instance port information.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="port" type="integer">
          Port value.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="type" type="string">
          Port type.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="productId" type="string">
      Product ID used to deploy the instance.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="productName" type="string">
      Product name used to deploy the instance.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="rootfsSize" type="integer">
      Root filesystem size (GB).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="volumeMounts" type="object[]">
      Instance storage configuration.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="type" type="string">
          Storage type. Values:

          * network: Cloud storage.
          * local: Local storage.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="size" type="string">
          Storage capacity.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="id" type="string">
          Cloud storage ID. Returned when type = network.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="mountPath" type="string">
          Storage mount path.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="statusError" type="object">
      Error information when instance creation fails or the instance is unavailable.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="state" type="string">
          Abnormal instance status.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="message" type="string">
          Error message.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="envs" type="object[]">
      Instance environment variable information.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="key" type="string">
          Environment variable name.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="value" type="string">
          Environment variable value.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="kind" type="string">
      Instance type.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="billingMode" type="string">
      Billing mode for the instance. Values:

      * onDemand: Pay-as-you-go billing.
      * monthly: Subscription (monthly or yearly) billing.
      * spot: Spot instance billing.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="endTime" type="string">
      Expiration time for subscription instances. For pay-as-you-go instances, returns -1.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="spotStatus" type="string">
      Spot instance status. Values:

      * `running`: Running
      * `notified`: Notified for reclaim
      * `reclaiming`: Being reclaimed
      * `terminated`: Terminated
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="spotReclaimTime" type="string">
      Spot instance reclaim time. Value "0" means reclaim has not started.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ParamField name="total" type="integer">
  Total number of results.
</ParamField>


# List Jobs
Source: https://novita.ai/docs/api-reference/gpu-instance-list-jobs

GET https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/jobs

## API Description

Retrieve a paginated list of background jobs for GPU instances. You can filter by job ID, state, type, time range, and creators.

## Request Headers

<ParamField type="string">
  Bearer authentication format, for example: Bearer \{\{API Key}}.
</ParamField>

## Query Parameters

<ParamField type="integer">
  Maximum number of items returned per page. Integer, value >= 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Page number to retrieve. Integer, value >= 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Filter by job ID. String, length 0–255.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Filter by job state. One of: `pulling` (preparing), `running`, `fail`, `success`, `break`.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Filter by job type. One of: `saveImage`, `instanceMigrate`, `autoInstanceMigrate`.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  Start of time range (Unix timestamp in seconds). Integer, value >= 0. Default: 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="integer">
  End of time range (Unix timestamp in seconds). Integer, value >= 0. Default: 0.
</ParamField>

<ParamField type="string">
  Filter by creator user ID.
</ParamField>

## cURL Example

```bash theme={"system"}
curl --location --request GET 'https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/jobs?pageSize=5&pageNum=1&jobId=&type=&state=&startTime=&endTime=&creators=' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer {{API_KEY}}'
```

## Response

<ResponseField name="jobs" type="object[]">
  Job list.

  <Expandable title="properties">
    <ResponseField name="Id" type="string">
      Job ID.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="user" type="string">
      The user ID who initiated the job.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="type" type="string">
      Job type. One of: `saveImage`, `instanceMigrate`, `autoInstanceMigrate`.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="envs" type="object[]">
      Environment variables for the job.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="key" type="string">
          Environment variable name.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="value" type="string">
          Environment variable value.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="state" type="object">
      Current job state information.

      <Expandable title="properties">
        <ResponseField name="state" type="string">
          Current job state. One of: `pulling`, `running`, `fail`, `success`, `break`.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="error" type="string">
          Error code, if any.
        </ResponseField>

        <ResponseField name="errorMessage" type="string">
          Error message, if any.
        </ResponseField>
      </Expandable>
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="logAddress" type="string">
      Log URL for viewing job execution logs.
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="createdAt" type="string">
      Job creation time (Unix timestamp in seconds).
    </ResponseField>

    <ResponseField name="instanceId" type="string">
      Associated instance ID when the job was executed.
    </ResponseField>
  </Expandable>
</ResponseField>

<ParamField name="total" type="integer">
  Total number of jobs matching the query.
</ParamField>


# List Network Storage

GET https://api.novita.ai/gpu-instance/openapi/v1/networkstorages/list
